titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 24, 2019 at 03:05

did.unittitle: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 69, 72 (Oversize)-73: Restricted fragile material. Contains glass. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 83, 86 (film): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 85 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 87 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Martin Greenblatt Foundation Book Fund in American Literature, 2014.

bioghist.head: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach
bioghist.p: Douglas Crase (1944-) is an American poet and writer. His husband, Frank Polach (1944-), is a poet, gardener, and former University Librarian at Rutgers, the State University of New Jersey.

bioghist.head: Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby
bioghist.p: Harry Dwight Dillon Ripley (1908-1973) was an English artist and patron of the arts associated with the New York School and the Tibor de Nagy Gallery. His life partner was Rupert Charles Barneby (1911-2000), an English botanist responsible for discovering over eleven hundred new species of plants.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Douglas Crase and Frank Polach papers contain writings, correspondence, photographs, research materials, artwork, and other papers by, to, or relating to the lives and work Douglas Crase and Frank Polach. Also includes extensive materials relating to the lives and work of Rupert Barnaby, Dwight Ripley, and Robert Dash.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Douglas Crase and Frank Polach Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: De Evia, Edgar - Ed White's Friend
De Leo, Hank - pen-and-ink for Doug and Frank
Democratic State Central Committee of Michigan
Diget, Patricia Denny - cousin David's 2d wife
Fulton, Alice and Hank de Leo
Handy, Cortlandt W. - Parker Handy Scholarship Donor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "A Garland for John Hollander: October 28, 1989"--assembled by Natalie Charkow and J.D. McClatchy--along with a poem by Douglas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "For James Merill: A Birthday Tribute," assembled by McClatchy for Merill's sixtieth birthday in 1986, which includes a poem by Douglas. A thank you note inscribed from Merrill in "Eight Bits" (folded sheet in wrappers) is laid in.

did.unittitle: Political Reform Commission of the Michigan Democratic Party

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Writings, card files, and related documentation
Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items. Including Crase college notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items. Including Crase college notebooks.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material from The Art and Romance of Dwight Ripley, Ruperti Imagines and Both.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material, drafts, and galleys from Both, as well as assorted magazines and publications.

did.unittitle: Manuscripts, drafts, research materials, correspondence, reviews and other related items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts and a record of submissions (index card box).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten journals and notebooks, along with notes and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings published in magazine, catalogs, anthologies, and books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Douglas Crase published in magazines, catalogs, anthologies, books, and other outlets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Marjorie Welish, John Ash, Trevor Winkfield, Deborah Rosenthal, Jed Perl, Arnold Kaufman, Douglas Crase Mark Bibbins, Robert Dash, and Margaret Fuller among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by John Koethe, Kenneth Koch, Thomas Spence Smith, Martin Earl, George Bradley, Tony Smith, and Ramon Gutierrez, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Nathan Kernan, John Koethe, David Lehman, Steven Meyer, Mary Kinzie, Susan Mitchell, Lawrence Joseph, Rachel Hadas, and Peter Haaren among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts, reviews, and other writings by Douglas Crase, John Ashbery, James Schulyer, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes genealogical and historical notes and research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes diplomas, award certificates, broadsides, and other personal papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a painting by Robert Dash, photographs, the May 4, 1976 issue of the The New York Times announcing Pulitzer prizes for the year (autographed by John Ashbery), along with other oversize materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This artwork depicts the view from James Schuyler's window at Chelsea Hotel--it is framed and inscribed to Douglas Crase. The drawing was used for the cover of James Schuyler and Darragh Park's Two Journals (Tibor de Nagy Editions, 1995).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between Frank Pollach and people including Georges Abawi, Bobbi Angell, and John Ashbery, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between Frank Pollach and many people including Susan Fraser, John Hawkins, James Hunter, Nathan Kernan, Nancy Litwin, Gerard Malanga, Budd Myers, Charles North, Charles, and John Yau, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes house, yard, and garden photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, menus, clippings, ephemera, and other documents, along with extensive notes and research about gardening and plants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes poems and other writings, along with notes, clippings, and other documents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of family and friends of Douglas Crase and Frank Pollach.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 mm color slides (4 slide trays - "Deep Space", "Sagg '76, '77, '78", "Sagg 80–81", [untitled].

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains A Family Landscape (1980) which is a 16 mm film with sound. Includes Chesebrough-Pond centennial promo filmed at Madoo in 1979 and scenes of Robert Dash painting. Filmscript is by Douglas Crase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs taken at Robert Dash's home in Sagaponack, Long Island; photographic negatives and contact sheets, snapshots from Frank and Douglas' Chelsea and Carley Brook homes and elsewhere; magazines and clippings about Robert Dash.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, writings, drawings, photographs, clippings, and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes, clippings, and photocopies collected during research projects performed by Frank Ripley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains printed material, photocopies, reviews, announcements, invitations, and drawings, and exhibition materials, much of it related to Ripley's artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three five-year diaries and ten "week-at-a-glance" calendars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Cabo de Gata," (1962), an ink and colored pencil artwork on paper by Dwight Ripley, framed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Setúbal," (1962), an ink and colored pencil artwork on paper by Dwight Ripley, framed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Ponty and Pinty," (1950) artwork by Dwight Ripley--typewritten poem with ink and colored pencil drawing with collaged element, framed. The drawing is on the back of an order form from a New York City tea store on East 55th Street. Also contains "Boom, No.1" (1951), an ink and gouache on stationery, framed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, notes, clippings, correspondence, and other materials primarily related to research performed by Barneby.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, notes, clippings, correspondence, and other materials primarily related to research performed by Barneby.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains framed photographs taken by Rupert Barneby including "Astragalus striatiflorus," (circa 1943–44) "Astragalus striatiflorus M. E. Jones on dune beneath sandstone cliffs, near Kenab, Utah," and "Cymopterus megacephalus," (circa 1941–45).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains framed and unframed artwork and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains framed photographs.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Rupert Barneby and Dwight Ripley at Frenchman Flat
Douglas Crase research materials on Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes and subject files about Antônio Francisco Lisboa, John Ashbery, Larry Rivers, James Schyuler, W.H. Auden, Christopher Isherwood, Stephen Spender, Grace Hartigan, Peggy Guggenheim, Patricia Holmgren, Judith Malina, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes and subject files related to Arthur Marshall, William Maas, Marie Menken, Stan Brakhage, Andy Warhol, Edward Albee, James Merrill, John Bernard Myers, Larry Rivers, James Schuyler, Alfonso A. Ossorio, Roland Pease, Édouard Roditi, Stanley L. Welsh, Keith Winter, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No Subnote Content

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes four albums containing photographs of England, Wappingers Falls, NY, Greenport, NY, the Mediterranean, California, and the Southwest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes four albums containing photographs of California and the Southwest, Stirling House Gardens in Greenport, NY, and other locations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains color photographs of Mexico, taken by Dwight Ripley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains color photographs of Mexico, taken by David Ripley.

did.unittitle: Photographs taken by Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four photograph albums.

did.unittitle: Photographs taken by Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two photograph albums.

did.unittitle: Books, awards, photographs, and other personal effects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three photographs of Louisa "Poppy" Geraldine (Ingham) Barneby, Rupert Barneby's mother.

did.unittitle: Ledger book contain handwritten record of species found by Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby
Rupert Barneby's Rolodex rotary card file
Citations, plaques and awards presented to Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains photographs and other materials.

did.unittitle: Citations, plaques, and awards presented to Rupert Barneby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a vasculum used in 1941 and later during the discovery Cymopterus ripleyi and other plants in Nevada.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an ornate vasculum used by Dwight Ripley and Rupert Barneby on expeditions to Spain and North Africa prior to 1939. It held many samples, including Limonium asparagoides brought back from Algeria.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains framed letter to Dwight Ripley from the Dylan Thomas Fund. Dec. 8. 1953. Letter is signed by Tennessee Williams, Thornton Wilder, Arthur Miller, Marianne Moore, W. H. Auden and Wallace Stevens.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Rupert Barneby and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains framed drawing by John Button, "Nude portrait of Joe LeSueur," circa early 1950s. Also contains framed letter to Dwight Ripley from Dylan Thomas Fund (1953) and a photograph of Dwight Ripley at the Plaza Hotel, circa 1944.

did.unittitle: Framed facsimile of a Tibor de Nagy Gallery ledger page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a framed pencil sketch by Dwight Ripley entitled "Master Rupert Barneby at Harrow" (1925) and a pencil sketch by Peter Markham Scott entitled "Rupert Barneby" (1937).
